label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out the answer to this question. The question is: If a person is bitten by a travel bug, he _ . The options are (A) prefers to stay at home, (B) feels a lot for pain, (C) is eager to see the world, or (D) hates exotic places. First, let me read the passage again to get the context. The passage starts by saying that some people prefer the comfort of home, while others have been "bitten by the travel bug" and can't wait to explore. It mentions exotic places calling to them and talks about all the different places people visit around the world. The main idea is that being bitten by the travel bug is a strong desire to travel and explore. So the key phrase here is "bitten by the travel bug." I know from common phrases that "travel bug" is an idiom meaning a strong desire to travel. So being "bitten by" it would mean that the person has caught that desire. The question is asking what that implies about the person. Looking at the options: (A) prefers to stay at home. But the passage says that people who are bitten by the travel bug are the opposite of those who prefer home. So A is probably wrong. (B) feels a lot for pain. The passage doesn't mention pain at all. The phrase "travel bug" doesn't relate to physical pain, so B seems off. (C) is eager to see the world. The passage says "others have been bitten by the travel bug and can't wait to explore the world." "Can't wait" and "eager" are synonyms here, so this seems correct. (D)
